title: Which Surfboard Is Better?
description: Comparatives and superlatives through Bia's surfboard shop visit. BIGGER, SMALLER, THE BEST — plus the tricky 'more + adjective' rule.
summary_pt_br: Comparativos (-er / more) e superlativos (-est / most) visitando a loja de pranchas. Regras de quando adicionar -er/-est e quando usar MORE/MOST. Irregulares: good/better/best, bad/worse/worst.

scenes.text_en:
Three boards on the wall. Three choices, three rides.
The first is LONGER and WIDER. It glides.
The second is SHORTER and FASTER and light.
The third is the MOST EXPENSIVE — and also the TIGHTEST.
scenes.text_pt:
Três pranchas na parede. Três opções, três caminhos.
A primeira é mais LONGA e mais LARGA. Ela plana bonito.
A segunda é mais CURTA, mais RÁPIDA, mais leve.
A terceira é a MAIS CARA — e também a mais apertadinha.

scenes.title: The comparative rule — short vs long adjectives
scenes.body_en: SHORT adjectives (1 syllable, or 2 ending in -y): add -ER / -EST. Big → bigger / biggest. Happy → happier / happiest. LONG adjectives (2+ syllables): use MORE / MOST. Expensive → more expensive / most expensive. Irregulars: good → better → best. Bad → worse → worst.
scenes.body_pt: Adjetivo CURTO (1 sílaba, ou 2 terminando em -y): usa -ER/-EST. Big → bigger/biggest. ADJETIVO LONGO (2+ sílabas): usa MORE/MOST. Expensive → more expensive/most expensive. Irregulares: good→better→best, bad→worse→worst.

examples.pt: alto → mais alto → o mais alto (curto)
lindo → mais lindo → o mais lindo (longo)

items.example_en: A truck is bigger than a car.
The whale is the biggest animal.

items.term: X is AS big AS Y
items.pt: X é tão grande quanto Y
items.example_en: Maya is as smart as Joca.

items.term: X is NOT AS big AS Y
items.pt: X não é tão grande quanto Y
items.example_en: This taco is not as hot as that one.

exercise.question: Complete: 'The red board is ___ than the blue one.' (fast)
Complete: 'The third board is the ___ expensive of all.'
exercise.explanation: EXPENSIVE is 3 syllables → MOST expensive. And 'the' before superlative.

exercise.question: Which is correct?
exercise.explanation: GOOD is irregular: good → BETTER → best. Never 'gooder' or 'more good'.
exercise.hint_pt_br: GOOD é irregular: good → better → best.

turns.en: Which is the best for a beginner?
turns.pt: Qual é a melhor pra iniciante?

turns.en: The longer one. It's slower — but more stable.
turns.pt: A mais longa. É mais lenta — mas mais estável.

turns.en: Is it as good as the expensive one?
turns.pt: É tão boa quanto a cara?

turns.en: For you? Better. Price is not the best teacher.
turns.pt: Pra você? Melhor. Preço não é o melhor professor.

exercise.explanation: Four irregulars worth memorizing. They break every short/long rule.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Quatro irregulares que quebram todas as regras. Decorar.

exercise.question: Maya is twelve. Bia is sixteen. Which sentence is correct?
options: Maya is as old as Bia
Maya is not as old as Bia
exercise.explanation: Maya is YOUNGER. So she is NOT as old as Bia. Negative with 'as...as'.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Maya é mais nova. Então 'not as old as' — negação do 'tão...quanto'.
